---
title: Local Government Expenditures by Category and Rurality
description: Horizontal faceted bar chart comparing local government expenditure by
  spending category across rural and nonrural counties.
date: '2026-05-14'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rwjf-seeds-of-change-public-funding-facet-expenditures-by-rurality-horiz-bar/
categories: []
tags: []
image: facet_expenditures_by_rurality_horiz_bar.png
chart:
  title: Local Government Expenditures by Category and Rurality
  chartType: bar
  interactive: false
  source: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Economic Research Service
  sourceUrl: https://www.ers.usda.gov/data-products/county-typology-codes/
  featured: false
producedBy: rwjf-seeds-of-change-public-funding
usesDatasets:
  - census-of-governments
  - bls-cpi-deflators
  - usda-county-typology
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Local Government Expenditures by Category and Rurality](facet_expenditures_by_rurality_horiz_bar.png)

## Overview

Compares per-capita local government expenditure across multiple spending categories for rural vs. nonrural counties, highlighting where the largest rural–nonrural disparities occur.

## Key Findings

- Education is the largest expenditure category in both rural and nonrural counties.
- Nonrural counties spend substantially more per capita on public welfare, health/hospitals, and transit.
- Rural counties spend proportionally more on utilities, highways, and natural resources relative to nonrural counties.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/final_viz/M4_expenditures_by_rurality_bar_horz.R` in the producing project.


::: {.callout-note}
## Dangling references

The following slugs are referenced by this project but do not yet have nodes in Dataverse. They are intentionally preserved as future content needs:

- `dataset/census-of-governments`
- `dataset/bls-cpi-deflators`
:::
